sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.94 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: If$G=[G,G]$, then the commutator width\/ of the group$G$is its width relative to the set of commutators. Let$V$be an infinite-dimensional vector space over a division ring. It is known that the commutator width of$GL(V)$is finite. Is it true that the commutator width of$GL(V)$is one? { V.\,Tolstykh}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
